heading: Volume cap
label: 146 Volume cap
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: A private activity bond issued as part of an issue meets the requirements of this section if the aggregate face amount of the private activity bonds issued pursuant to such issue, when added to the aggregate face amount of tax-exempt private activity bonds previously issued by the issuing authority during the calendar year, does not exceed such authority’s volume cap for such calendar year.

children.heading: Volume cap for State agencies
children.label: b Volume cap for State agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The volume cap for any agency of the State authorized to issue tax-exempt private activity bonds for any calendar year shall be 50 percent of the State ceiling for such calendar year.

children.heading: Special rule where State has more than 1 agency
children.label: 2 Special rule where State has more than 1 agency
children.text: If more than 1 agency of the State is authorized to issue tax-exempt private activity bonds, all such agencies shall be treated as a single agency.

children.heading: Volume cap for other issuers
children.label: c Volume cap for other issuers
A
children.text: the population of the jurisdiction of such issuing authority, bears to

children.label: B
children.text: the population of the entire State.

children.heading: Overlapping jurisdictions
children.label: 2 Overlapping jurisdictions
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A), if an area is within the jurisdiction of 2 or more governmental units, such area shall be treated as only within the jurisdiction of the unit having jurisdiction over the smallest geographical area unless such unit agrees to surrender all or part of such jurisdiction for such calendar year to the unit with overlapping jurisdiction which has the next smallest geographical area.

children.label: A
children.text: an amount equal to $75 ($62.50 in the case of calendar year 2001) multiplied by the State population, or

children.label: B
children.text: $225,000,000 ($187,500,000 in the case of calendar year 2001).

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for such calendar year by substituting “calendar year 2001” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Special rule for States with constitutional home rule cities
children.label: 3 Special rule for States with constitutional home rule cities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The volume cap for any constitutional home rule city for any calendar year shall be determined under paragraph (1) of subsection (c) by substituting “100 percent” for “50 percent”.

children.heading: Coordination with other allocations
children.label: B Coordination with other allocations
children.text: In the case of any State which contains 1 or more constitutional home rule cities, for purposes of applying subsections (b) and (c) with respect to issuing authorities in such State other than constitutional home rule cities, the State ceiling for any calendar year shall be reduced by the aggregate volume caps determined for such year for all constitutional home rule cities in such State.

children.heading: Constitutional home rule city
children.label: C Constitutional home rule city
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “constitutional home rule city” means, with respect to any calendar year, any political subdivision of a State which, under a State constitution which was adopted in 1970 and effective on , had home rule powers on the 1st day of the calendar year.July 1, 1971

children.heading: Special rule for possessions with populations of less than the population of the least populous State
children.label: 4 Special rule for possessions with populations of less than the population of the least populous State

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the population of any possession of the United States for any calendar year is less than the population of the least populous State (other than a possession) for such calendar year, the limitation under paragraph (1)(A) shall not be less than the amount determined under subparagraph (B) for such calendar year.

children.label: I
children.text: the numerator of which is the amount applicable under paragraph (1)(B) for such calendar year, and

children.label: II
children.text: the denominator of which is the State population of the least populous State (other than a possession) for such calendar year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the population of such possession for such calendar year.

children.heading: Increase and set aside for housing bonds for 2008
children.label: 5 Increase and set aside for housing bonds for 2008
i
children.text: the numerator of which is the State ceiling applicable to the State for calendar year 2008, determined without regard to this paragraph, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the denominator of which is the sum of the State ceilings determined under clause (i) for all States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any amount of the State ceiling for any State which is attributable to an increase under this paragraph shall be allocated solely for one or more qualified housing issues.

children.label: I
children.text: an issue described in section 142(a)(7) (relating to qualified residential rental projects), or

children.label: II
children.text: a qualified mortgage issue (determined by substituting “12-month period” for “42-month period” each place it appears in section 143(a)(2)(D)(i)).

children.heading: State may provide for different allocation
children.label: e State may provide for different allocation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), a State may, by law provide a different formula for allocating the State ceiling among the governmental units (or other authorities) in such State having authority to issue tax-exempt private activity bonds.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraph (3), the Governor of any State may proclaim a different formula for allocating the State ceiling among the governmental units (or other authorities) in such State having authority to issue private activity bonds.

children.label: i
children.text: the last day of the 1st calendar year after 1986 during which the legislature of the State met in regular session, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the effective date of any State legislation with respect to the allocation of the State ceiling.

children.heading: State may not alter allocation to constitutional home rule cities
children.label: 3 State may not alter allocation to constitutional home rule cities
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in a State constitutional amendment (or law changing the home rule provision adopted in the manner provided by the State constitution), the authority provided in this subsection shall not apply to that portion of the State ceiling which is allocated to any constitutional home rule city in the State unless such city agrees to such different allocation.

children.heading: Elective carryforward of unused limitation for specified purpose
children.label: f Elective carryforward of unused limitation for specified purpose
A
children.text: an issuing authority’s volume cap for any calendar year after 1985, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount of tax-exempt private activity bonds issued during such calendar year by such authority,

children.label: A
children.text: identify the purpose for which the carryforward is elected, and

children.label: B
children.text: specify the portion of the excess described in paragraph (1) which is to be a carryforward for each such purpose.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If any issuing authority elects a carryforward under paragraph (1) with respect to any carryforward purpose, any private activity bonds issued by such authority with respect to such purpose during the 3 calendar years following the calendar year in which the carryforward arose shall not be taken into account under subsection (a) to the extent the amount of such bonds does not exceed the amount of the carryforward elected for such purpose.

children.heading: Order in which carryforward used
children.label: B Order in which carryforward used
children.text: Carryforwards elected with respect to any purpose shall be used in the order of the calendar years in which they arose.

children.heading: Election
children.label: 4 Election
children.text: Any election under this paragraph (and any identification or specification contained therein), once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.label: A
children.text: the purpose of issuing exempt facility bonds described in 1 of the paragraphs of section 142(a),

children.label: B
children.text: the purpose of issuing qualified mortgage bonds or mortgage credit certificates,

children.label: C
children.text: the purpose of issuing qualified student loan bonds, and

children.label: D
children.text: the purpose of issuing qualified redevelopment bonds.

children.heading: Special rules for increased volume cap under subsection (d)(5)
children.label: 6 Special rules for increased volume cap under subsection (d)(5)
A
children.text: for any issue other than a qualified housing issue (as defined in subsection (d)(5)), or

children.label: B
children.text: to issue any bond after calendar year 2010.
any qualified veterans’ mortgage bond,
any qualified 501(c)(3) bond,
any exempt facility bond issued as part of an issue described in paragraph (1), (2), (12), (13), (14), or (15) of section 142(a),
75 percent of any exempt facility bond issued as part of an issue described in paragraph (11) of section 142(a) (relating to high-speed intercity rail facilities),
75 percent of any exempt facility bond issued as part of an issue described in paragraph (16) of section 142(a) (relating to qualified broadband projects), and
75 percent of any exempt facility bond issued as part of an issue described in paragraph (17) of section 142(a) (relating to qualified carbon dioxide capture facilities).

children.heading: Exception for government-owned solid waste disposal facilities
children.label: h Exception for government-owned solid waste disposal facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Only for purposes of this section, the term “private activity bond” shall not include any exempt facility bond described in section 142(a)(6) which is issued as part of an issue if all of the property to be financed by the net proceeds of such issue is to be owned by a governmental unit.

children.heading: Safe harbor for determination of government ownership
children.label: 2 Safe harbor for determination of government ownership
children.text: In determining ownership for purposes of paragraph (1), section 142(b)(1)(B) shall apply, except that a lease term shall be treated as satisfying clause (ii) thereof if it is not more than 20 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “private activity bond” shall not include any bond which is issued to refund another bond to the extent that the amount of such bond does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded bond.

children.heading: Special rules for student loan bonds
children.label: 2 Special rules for student loan bonds
A
children.text: the average maturity date of the qualified student loan bonds to be refunded by the issue of which the refunding bond is a part, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date 17 years after the date on which the refunded bond was issued (or in the case of a series of refundings, the date on which the original bond was issued).

children.heading: Special rules for qualified mortgage bonds
children.label: 3 Special rules for qualified mortgage bonds
A
children.text: the average maturity date of the qualified mortgage bonds to be refunded by the issue of which the refunding bond is a part, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date 32 years after the date on which the refunded bond was issued (or in the case of a series of refundings, the date on which the original bond was issued).

children.heading: Average maturity
children.label: 4 Average maturity
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (2) and (3), average maturity shall be determined in accordance with section 147(b)(2)(A).

children.heading: Exception for advance refunding
children.label: 5 Exception for advance refunding
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any bond issued to advance refund another bond.

children.heading: Treatment of certain residential rental project bonds as refunding bonds irrespective of obligor
children.label: 6 Treatment of certain residential rental project bonds as refunding bonds irrespective of obligor

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If, during the 6-month period beginning on the date of a repayment of a loan financed by an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are used to provide projects described in section 142(d), such repayment is used to provide a new loan for any project so described, any bond which is issued to refinance such issue shall be treated as a refunding issue to the extent the principal amount of such refunding issue does not exceed the principal amount of the bonds refunded.

children.label: i
children.text: the refunding issue is issued not later than 4 years after the date on which the original issue was issued,

children.label: ii
children.text: the latest maturity date of any bond of the refunding issue is not later than 34 years after the date on which the refunded bond was issued, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the refunding issue is approved in accordance with section 147(f) before the issuance of the refunding issue.

children.heading: Population
children.label: j Population
children.text: For purposes of this section, determinations of the population of any State (or issuing authority) shall be made with respect to any calendar year on the basis of the most recent census estimate of the resident population of such State (or issuing authority) released by the Bureau of Census before the beginning of such calendar year.

children.heading: Facility must be located within State
children.label: k Facility must be located within State

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), no portion of the State ceiling applicable to any State for any calendar year may be used with respect to financing for a facility located outside such State.

children.heading: Exception for certain facilities where State will get proportionate share of benefits
children.label: 2 Exception for certain facilities where State will get proportionate share of benefits
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any exempt facility bond described in paragraph (4), (5), (6), or (10) of section 142(a) if the issuer establishes that the State’s share of the use of the facility (or its output) will equal or exceed the State’s share of the private activity bonds issued to finance the facility.

children.heading: Treatment of governmental bonds to which volume cap allocated
children.label: 3 Treatment of governmental bonds to which volume cap allocated
A
children.text: for an output facility, or

children.label: B
children.text: for a facility of a type described in paragraph (4), (5), (6), or (10) of section 142(a),

children.heading: Issuer of qualified scholarship funding bonds
children.label: l Issuer of qualified scholarship funding bonds
children.text: In the case of a qualified scholarship funding bond, such bond shall be treated for purposes of this section as issued by a State or local issuing authority (whichever is appropriate).

children.heading: Treatment of amounts allocated to private activity portion of government use bonds
children.label: m Treatment of amounts allocated to private activity portion of government use bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The volume cap of an issuer shall be reduced by the amount allocated by the issuer to an issue under section 141(b)(5).

children.heading: Advance refundings
children.label: 2 Advance refundings
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by the Secretary, any advance refunding of any part of an issue to which an amount was allocated under section 141(b)(5) (or would have been allocated if such section applied to such issue) shall be taken into account under this section to the extent of the amount of the volume cap which was (or would have been) so allocated.

children.heading: Reduction for mortgage credit certificates, etc.
children.label: n Reduction for mortgage credit certificates, etc.

children.text: the amount of qualified mortgage bonds which such authority elects not to issue under section 25(c)(2)(A)(ii) during such year, plus
the amount of any reduction in such ceiling under section 25(f) applicable to such authority for such year.
